ECMAScript 2026 (ES2026) represents one of the most significant updates to JavaScript in years. After nearly a decade of development, several long-awaited features have finally reached Stage 4 and are now part of the official specification.
The three major additions we’ll cover are:
i) Temporal API: A modern replacement for the notoriously problematic Date object
ii) Decorators: A powerful metaprogramming feature for classes
iii) New utility methods: Including Math.sumPrecise(), Iterator.concat(), Promise.try() and more
Let’s dive deep into each of these features with practical code examples.
1. The Temporal API: Fixing JavaScript Dates
The Problem with JavaScript’s Date Object
For nearly 30 years, JavaScript developers have struggled with the Date object. Here are its most notorious issues:
// Problem 1: Months are 0-indexed (January = 0)
const date = new Date(2026, 0, 15); // January 15, not February!// Problem 2: Mutability causes bugs
const meeting = new Date('2026-03-21');
const reminder = meeting;
reminder.setDate(reminder.getDate() - 1); // Oops…
